# PolyBot Platform Documentation

> 15-document specification for an automated Polymarket trading platform.
> Python 3.11+ async microservices with Docker Compose deployment on VPS.
> MVP strategy: Binary Arbitrage (Full-Set Parity).

## Docs

- [Product Research](./01-product-research.md): Market analysis, Polymarket ecosystem, competitor landscape, risk assessment, technology evaluation
  - Executive Summary (L5-9) [overview, opportunity]
  - Problem Statement (L11-57) [market problem, personas, current alternatives]
  - Market Analysis (L59-102) [TAM, SAM, SOM, trends, timing]
  - Competitive Landscape (L104-135) [competitors, substitutes, competitive advantage]
  - Risk Assessment (L137-181) [market risks, technical risks, business model risks, oracle manipulation]
  - Technology Ecosystem Assessment (L183-237) [SDK maturity, platform constraints]
  - Recommendation (L210-256) [go/no-go verdict, conditions]

- [Product Roadmap](./02-product-roadmap.md): 6-phase roadmap from Binary Arb MVP through AI/ML ensemble, wallet architecture, milestones, decision log
  - Vision & Strategic Pillars (L5-19) [vision statement, 5 strategic pillars]
  - Wallet Architecture (L21-87) [3-tier risk model, Vault/Alpha/Sweep, phase rollout]
  - Phase 1: Foundation + Binary Arb MVP (L89-186) [weeks 1-6, features, sub-phases, success metrics, exit criteria]
  - Phase 2: Market Making (L188-242) [weeks 7-12, spread farming, volatility]
  - Phase 3: Latency Arbitrage (L244-290) [weeks 13-20, Rust sidecar, <100ms]
  - Phase 4: Copy Trading (L292-335) [weeks 21-26, whale tracking, on-chain]
  - Phase 5: NegRisk Arbitrage (L337-381) [weeks 27-32, multi-outcome, conversion graph]
  - Phase 6: AI/ML Ensemble (L383-434) [weeks 33-44, LLM pipeline, cross-platform]
  - Technical Debt Budget (L436-453) [per-phase allocation]
  - Risk Registry (L455-487) [cross-phase risks]
  - Decision Log (L471-527) [key decisions with rationale]
  - Budget Estimates (L529-553) [infrastructure costs, trading capital]

- [PRD](./03-prd.md): 63 user stories across 9 epics, functional/non-functional requirements, acceptance criteria
  - Overview (L3-15) [product metadata]
  - Goals & Non-Goals (L17-39) [8 goals, 8 non-goals]
  - User Personas (L41-67) [operator, strategy developer, risk manager]
  - Epic 1: Bot Framework (L71-82) [US-001 to US-005, BaseBot interface]
  - Epic 2: Orchestration (L84-103) [US-006 to US-010, lifecycle management]
  - Epic 3: Market Data (L105-115) [US-011 to US-014, WebSocket, Gamma API]
  - Epic 4: Execution (L117-126) [US-015 to US-019, CLOB, rate limiting, batching]
  - Epic 5: Risk Management (L128-139) [US-020 to US-024, circuit breakers, emergency stop]
  - Epic 6: Wallet Management (L141-151) [US-025 to US-029, multi-EOA, software ledger]
  - Epic 7: Dashboard (L153-163) [US-030 to US-035, React SPA, SSE]
  - Epic 8: Binary Arbitrage Bot (L165-179) [US-036 to US-040, full-set parity]
  - Epic 9: Observability (L181-210) [US-041 to US-048, US-905 to US-909: metrics, alerts, logging, debug mode, drawdown, paper graduation]
  - Functional Requirements (L211-237) [FR-1 through FR-6]
  - Non-Functional Requirements (L239-264) [performance, availability, scalability, security]
  - Out of Scope (L266-293) [deferred features]
  - Open Questions (L295-306) [unresolved decisions]

- [Technical Specification](./04-technical-specification.md): Architecture, 6 ADRs, data model (ER diagram), 6 service designs, Redis topology, binary arb algorithm, simulation-first policy
  - System Context Diagram (L5-28) [Mermaid diagram, external boundaries]
  - High-Level Architecture (L30-102) [Mermaid diagram, component interactions]
  - ADRs (L104-153) [ADR-001 to ADR-006: Python, PostgreSQL+TimescaleDB, Redis Streams, wallet tiers, FastAPI+React, BaseBot ABC]
  - Technology Stack (L156-181) [full stack table with versions and rationale]
  - Data Model / ER Diagram (L183-403) [Mermaid ER, 15+ entities, hypertables, continuous aggregates]
  - Service 1: Market Data (L407-452) [WebSocket manager, Gamma polling, Redis Streams publishing]
  - Service 2: Orchestrator (L454-559) [bot loader, lifecycle FSM, health checks, simulation-first policy, paper mode enforcement]
  - Service 3: Execution Engine (L561-622) [py-clob-client wrapper, rate limiter, order state machine, batching, reconciliation]
  - Service 4: Risk Manager (L624-712) [10-step pre-trade pipeline, drawdown check, per-trade max loss, circuit breaker FSM, emergency stop, risk parameter hierarchy with global/strategy/bot levels]
  - Service 5: Wallet Manager (L714-815) [3-tier model, software ledger, per-wallet mutex, rebalancing]
  - Service 6: Dashboard API (L817-864) [FastAPI routes, SSE publishing, React SPA]
  - Integration Architecture (L866-944) [Polymarket API map, auth flow, Redis topology]
  - Scalability Design (L946-979) [horizontal scaling path, VPS resource budget]
  - Error Handling & Resilience (L981-1008) [failure modes, retry policies]
  - Observability (L1010-1112) [logging strategy, bot debug mode (events table, per-bot JSONL), Prometheus metrics, alerting thresholds]
  - Binary Arb Strategy (L1115-1186) [algorithm flowchart, market pair discovery, profit calculation]

- [Development Guidelines](./05-development-guidelines.md): Repo structure, dev setup, Git workflow with STATE.md update rule, code standards, CI/CD with UAT gate, performance budget
  - Repository Structure (L3-182) [full directory tree with explanations]
  - Development Environment Setup (L184-266) [prerequisites, setup steps, dev vs prod, Makefile commands]
  - Git Workflow (L268-352) [GitHub Flow, branch naming, conventional commits with `state` scope, STATE.md update on commit rule, PR requirements]
  - Code Standards (L354-424) [Python (ruff, mypy, structlog), TypeScript/React]
  - Testing Requirements (L426-476) [coverage targets, critical test cases, fixtures]
  - CI/CD Pipeline (L478-561) [pipeline stages with UAT gate, GitHub Actions workflow, user acceptance section, deployment]
  - Documentation Standards (L563-579) [what to document, where]
  - Dependency Management (L581-621) [adding deps, pinning, security scanning]
  - Performance Budget (L623-666) [Core Web Vitals, trading latency, API latency]

- [CLAUDE.md](./06-claude-md.md): Ready-to-copy CLAUDE.md for coding agents — feature delivery protocol, session start protocol, build/test commands, architecture summary, 13 gotchas
  - Feature Delivery Protocol (L11-20) [MANDATORY iterative cycle: implement → demo → user approval]
  - Session Start Protocol (L22-40) [MANDATORY: read STATE.md + git log, announce orientation, first-session creation from template]
  - Documentation Reference Table (L42-68) [which doc to read for each task area, fast lookup hierarchy]
  - Build / Run / Test Commands (L72-98) [make dev, docker compose, pytest, ruff, mypy, alembic, frontend]
  - Architecture Overview (L100-113) [6 services + bot modules, Redis + PostgreSQL]
  - Key Directories (L115-125) [docs, src/core, src/services, src/bots, src/shared, config, frontend]
  - Critical Patterns (L127-158) [BaseBot interface, order submission flow, wallet architecture]
  - Gotchas (L160-186) [FOK decimals, WS 500 limit, dynamic fees, per-wallet rate limits, Redis Streams, emergency stop, Pydantic v2, TimescaleDB, Alembic, py-clob-client, debug mode volume, paper-first policy, 10-step pipeline]
  - Environment Variables (L188-203) [.env template]
  - Conventions (L205-212) [commit format, branch naming, async-only, structlog, Prometheus]
  - MCP Servers (L214-227) [local-rag, Context7, when to use which]

- [Agents & Skills](./07-agents-md.md): AGENTS.md with 5 roles, 10 slash commands, 4 sub-agent configs, 7 MCP servers, development workflows with UAT checkpoints and STATE.md updates
  - Fast Documentation Lookup (L14-21) [4-layer navigation hierarchy: llms.txt → /docs skill → mcp-local-rag → direct read]
  - Documentation Quick Reference (L23-34) [task → document routing table]
  - Rules for Using Documentation (L36-41) [4 rules for agents]
  - Agent Roles (L43-93) [core-architect, service-dev, bot-dev, frontend-dev, infra-ops]
  - Slash Commands (L96-425) [/new-bot, /check-risk, /test-strategy, /deploy, /debug-bot, /e2e-test, /wallet-audit, /market-scan, /perf-profile, /backtest]
  - MCP Server Configuration (L429-480) [5 P0 servers (local-rag, context7, postgres, redis, docker) + 2 P1 (grafana, sequential-thinking), when to use which tool]
  - Development Workflows (L483-535) [new service feature, new bot strategy, infrastructure changes — all with ⏸ DEMO TO USER checkpoints and STATE.md update steps]
  - STATE.md Maintenance Rule (L537-547) [every commit must update STATE.md, combined vs separate commit options]
  - Compatibility Notes (L549-573) [Claude Code, opencode, Cursor/Windsurf]

- [Security Specification](./08-security-spec.md): Threat model (STRIDE), auth, data protection, API security, incident response
  - Threat Model (L3-51) [assets, threat actors, attack surface, STRIDE analysis]
  - Authentication & Authorization (L53-90) [Polymarket API auth (L2Auth + HMAC), dashboard Bearer token, authorization model]
  - Data Protection (L92-143) [encryption at rest/transit, PII handling, log redaction patterns]
  - API Security (L145-173) [rate limiting, input validation, CORS policy]
  - Infrastructure Security (L175-225) [network segmentation, Docker network, secrets management, VPS hardening checklist]
  - Compliance (L227-247) [GDPR/SOC2/HIPAA assessment, Polymarket-specific]
  - Security Development Lifecycle (L248-271) [SAST tools, code review checklist]
  - Incident Response (L273-331) [severity levels, emergency procedures]
  - Security Checklist (L313-341) [pre-launch verification]

- [Infrastructure Specification](./09-infrastructure-spec.md): Docker Compose, VPS setup, Caddy, Prometheus/Grafana, backup/recovery
  - Docker Compose Production (L13-417) [full docker-compose.yml with 11 services, health checks, resource limits]
  - Docker Compose Dev (L419-464) [dev overrides, hot reload]
  - Dockerfile Pattern (L466-535) [base image, multi-stage build, port assignments]
  - Network Architecture (L537-646) [topology diagram, firewall rules (UFW), Caddy reverse proxy config]
  - VPS Provisioning (L648-831) [hardware requirements, full setup-vps.sh script]
  - Secrets Management (L833-907) [.env structure, sops/age encryption, rotation schedule]
  - Monitoring Stack (L909-1105) [Prometheus config, alert rules (trading/performance/infra), Grafana dashboards]
  - Backup & Recovery (L1107-1288) [backup strategy, backup.sh script, restore.sh script, cron config]
  - Deployment Workflow (L1290-1386) [initial deployment, rolling updates, rollback]
  - Database Management (L1388-1464) [PostgreSQL config, TimescaleDB retention/compression, storage projections]
  - Redis Configuration (L1466-1512) [memory management, stream trimming, memory budget]
  - Log Management (L1514-1546) [log architecture, rotation, aggregation]
  - Health Checks (L1548-1601) [health contract, matrix, dependency chain]
  - Operational Procedures (L1603-1658) [daily checklist, emergency stop, service restart]
  - Capacity Planning (L1660-1685) [scaling triggers, phase-based evolution]
  - Disaster Recovery (L1687-1735) [RTO/RPO, full DR procedure, position reconciliation]
  - Security Infrastructure (L1736-1773) [container security, image scanning, network security]
  - Makefile Reference (L1775-1887) [all make targets]

- [API Specification](./10-api-specification.md): Dashboard REST + SSE API, Polymarket API integration, Redis communication, Pydantic models
  - Common Conventions (L14-113) [base URL, auth, response envelope, error codes, pagination, filtering]
  - System Endpoints (L115-239) [health, status, emergency-stop, resume]
  - Bot Management (L241-555) [CRUD, lifecycle (start/stop/pause/resume), state machine]
  - Trade History (L557-648) [trades list, summary with P&L]
  - Positions (L650-684) [active positions with unrealized P&L]
  - Risk Endpoints (L686-788) [overview, circuit breakers, risk events]
  - Wallet Endpoints (L790-855) [wallet list, ledger entries]
  - Market Endpoints (L857-941) [markets list, orderbook snapshots]
  - Settings (L943-1023) [get/patch global settings]
  - Signals (L1025-1062) [signal history]
  - Audit Log (L1064-1095) [audit trail]
  - SSE Event Stream (L1097-1195) [7 event types, React implementation example]
  - Polymarket API Integration (L1197-1466) [CLOB REST auth, order submission, WebSocket, Gamma API, Data API, rate limits, error handling]
  - Inter-Service Redis Communication (L1468-1508) [Streams, Pub/Sub, Cache schemas]
  - Pydantic Models (L1510-1573) [OrderBookSnapshot, Signal, OrderRequest, FillEvent, RiskCheckResult, HealthStatus]

- [Testing Strategy](./11-testing-strategy.md): Test pyramid, 14 critical test suites, drawdown/loss tests, paper trading graduation (mandatory), coverage requirements
  - Overview & Test Pyramid (L3-24) [unit ~200+, integration ~40, E2E ~10]
  - Unit Tests (L28-254) [14 critical suites with code examples: circuit breaker, pre-trade risk, binary arb strategy, order state machine, fee calculator, rate limiter, position tracker, wallet router, config validation, signal generator, websocket manager, bot lifecycle, emergency stop, pnl calculator]
  - Integration Tests (L256-358) [execution flow, bot lifecycle, wallet routing, emergency stop — with code examples]
  - E2E Tests (L360-378) [paper trading, emergency stop, dashboard, config change, recovery]
  - Paper Trading Tests (L380-411) [mode design, validation checklist]
  - Frontend Tests (L412-458) [component, hook, E2E tests]
  - Test Infrastructure (L460-585) [fixtures (conftest.py patterns), JSON fixture files]
  - Test Execution (L587-636) [running tests, CI pipeline integration]
  - Coverage Requirements (L638-661) [per-module targets: core 80%, risk 80%, execution 80%, bots 70%, services 60%, frontend 60%]
  - Quality Gates (L663-703) [pre-commit checks, PR merge requirements]
  - Specialized Testing (L705-905) [financial precision, concurrency, resilience, drawdown protection, per-trade loss limit tests]
  - Test Environments (L909-917) [unit, integration, paper, staging, production]
  - Paper Trading → Production Graduation (L919-981) [MANDATORY 3-stage path with operator sign-off gates, validation criteria tables, production ramp schedule]

- [Project Governance](./12-project-governance.md): Decision-making, iterative contribution workflow with UAT + STATE.md, release management, bot deployment governance, team scaling
  - Decision-Making Framework (L11-51) [authority levels, ADR template, decision log]
  - Contribution Workflow (L53-186) [agent-assisted human-in-the-loop workflow, 9-step feature delivery protocol (step 9: STATE.md update), demo template, self-review checklist with STATE.md check, team workflow, PR template, code review guidelines]
  - Release Management (L189-256) [semantic versioning, release process, changelog format]
  - Branching Strategy (L257-304) [GitHub Flow, branch naming, hotfix process]
  - Issue Tracking (L307-354) [labels, issue template]
  - Operational Governance (L357-447) [config change rules, bot deployment governance (paper → staging → production stage gates), post-mortem template]
  - Documentation Governance (L450-481) [ownership matrix (includes STATE.md), quality rules]
  - Security Governance (L482-515) [access control, secrets handling, dependency security]
  - Team Scaling Guide (L516-566) [adding contributor #2, #3-5, CODEOWNERS]
  - Compliance & Audit (L568-596) [audit trail, Polymarket compliance]
  - Communication (L598-632) [internal comms, alert routing]

- [Claude Code Setup Guide](./13-claude-code-setup.md): VPS setup for Claude Code with 5 P0 MCP servers, 10 slash commands, 4 sub-agents, STATE.md, and doc navigation
  - Quick Start (L5-50) [Node.js install, Claude Code CLI, file setup]
  - File Placement (L69-108) [copy CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .mcp.json, skills, commands, agents, STATE.md]
  - MCP Server Setup (L111-165) [5 P0 servers: local-rag, context7, postgres, redis, docker + 2 P1: grafana, sequential-thinking]
  - MCP Connection Requirements (L165-176) [which servers need Docker Compose running]
  - Verification Tests (L177-235) [7 verification steps including new MCP servers]
  - Documentation Navigation (L237-266) [4-layer hierarchy, when to use each layer]
  - MCP Server Details (L268-325) [mcp-local-rag, Context7 detailed config]
  - Troubleshooting (L327-381) [common issues and fixes]
  - Quick Reference Card (L383-432) [cheat sheet with all 10 slash commands]

- [MCP & Skills Specification](./14-mcp-skills-specification.md): 7 MCP servers, 10 slash commands, phase-gated rollout, context window budget
  - Design Principles [don't over-tool, phase-gated, skills wrap MCPs]
  - MCP Server Inventory — Existing (L20-50) [local-rag, context7]
  - MCP Server Inventory — P0 (L52-140) [postgres (@crystaldba/postgres-mcp), redis (@redis/mcp-redis), docker (@quantgeekdev/docker-mcp)]
  - MCP Server Inventory — P1 (L142-180) [grafana (@grafana/mcp-grafana), sequential-thinking]
  - Already-Connected Assessment (L182-210) [Playwright: HIGH VALUE, Supabase: NOT for PolyBot]
  - NOT Recommended (L212-225) [Filesystem, GitHub, WebSocket MCPs]
  - Context Window Budget (L227-245) [Phase 1: ~5,900 tokens, Phase 2: ~8,000 tokens]
  - P0 Slash Commands (L247-380) [/new-bot, /check-risk, /test-strategy, /deploy, /debug-bot]
  - P1 Slash Commands (L382-450) [/e2e-test, /wallet-audit, /market-scan, /perf-profile]
  - P2 Slash Commands (L452-480) [/backtest]
  - Phase-Gated Rollout (L482-510) [Phase 1: MVP, Phase 2: Growth, Phase 3: Advanced]
  - Future Considerations (L512+) [wallet sub-agent split, AI/ML agent, Rust sidecar MCP]

- [STATE.md Template](./15-state-template.md): Template for STATE.md project state file — session continuity between agent sessions
  - Template (L1-57) [STATE.md format: current focus, completed table, in-progress checklist, up next, blockers, session notes]
  - Usage Instructions (L60-92) [when to create, how to maintain on every commit, token budget target <500]
